Let me check what actually changed and try clicking on different things - maybe the UI bars below the checkerboard, or the blue player character.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed between previous and current frame
# The result said 1 cell changed at bbox [1, 61, 1, 61] - that's the top blue bar (timer)
# Let's verify the board is otherwise the same
seg_before = None
seg_after = current_frame.segmentation

# Check if any gameplay objects changed by comparing key objects
print("Current key objects:")
for n in seg_after['nodes']:
    if n['id'] in [0, 2]:
        continue
    if n['pixels'] == 16 and n['color'] in ['B', 'c']:
        continue
    # Get center of boundary
    b = n['boundary']
    center_row = sum(p[0] for p in b) / len(b)
    center_col = sum(p[1] for p in b) / len(b)
    print(f"  id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} center=({center_row:.0f}, {center_col:.0f})")

# The small bars below the frame - let's click on the first black one (id=77 area)
# id=77: B pixels=3 at row 42, col 20-22
# Let's try clicking on the first UI element (black bar at row 42, col 21)
print("\nTrying to click on first UI bar (black, row 42, col 21)")
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 42, 'col': 21}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
